_meta.purpose: WP-TC1 conflict-lens precision evaluation ground truth. Authored by Vince (manager) 2026-07-15 — labeler independent of the implementer by design. Check in at tests/fixtures/conflict_lens_labeled_pairs.json.

label_semantics.contradicts: The lens SHOULD propose contradicts A<->B: both nodes assert incompatible stances on the same subject, both current/HEAD.
label_semantics.supersedes: The lens SHOULD propose supersedes (newer->older) INSTEAD of contradicts: same decision lineage, stance evolved.
label_semantics.none: The lens should propose NOTHING for this pair. Proposing contradicts on a 'none' or 'supersedes' pair is a false positive against the precision bar.

_meta.scoring: precision(contradicts) = true contradicts proposals / all contradicts proposals. Bar >= 0.9. Recall reported, not gated.

pairs.label: contradicts
pairs.rationale: Directly opposed rules on the same subject (retry policy), different authors, both HEAD.

a.summary: HTTP client retries: always retry idempotent calls 3x with exponential backoff
a.detail: Chosen for resilience against transient network faults. Rejected: fail-fast (too flaky in prod).

b.summary: Never retry HTTP calls at the client layer — retries belong exclusively to the gateway
b.detail: Client-layer retries caused duplicate writes during the March incident. All retry logic centralizes in the gateway.

pairs.label: contradicts
pairs.rationale: Pinned value conflict: same config key, two different mandated values.

a.summary: Connection pool size MUST stay at 10 — DB tier licence caps concurrent connections

b.summary: Raise connection pool to 50 for the reporting batch window
b.detail: Reporting jobs starve at 10 connections; bump to 50 during the nightly window.

pairs.label: contradicts
pairs.rationale: Do-X vs never-X on the same file/practice.

a.summary: Always regenerate the lockfile in the same commit as a dependency bump
a.detail: Split bumps caused unreproducible builds twice.

b.summary: Lockfile regeneration happens only in weekly maintenance commits, never in feature commits
b.detail: Keeps feature diffs reviewable; lockfile churn batches weekly.

pairs.label: contradicts
pairs.rationale: Storage-format stance conflict, both current, different authors.

a.summary: Timestamps stored as UTC ISO-8601 strings everywhere in the journal

b.summary: New journal entries write epoch milliseconds for timestamps to cut parse cost
b.detail: ISO parsing dominated replay profile; epoch ints are 4x faster.

pairs.label: contradicts
pairs.rationale: Rule vs blanket exception that swallows the rule.

a.summary: No network calls in unit tests — hermetic suite, high severity
a.detail: CI has no egress; any network call flakes.

b.summary: Unit tests hit the live staging API for realistic fixtures — record/replay was too brittle
b.detail: Staging is stable enough; live calls keep fixtures honest.

pairs.label: contradicts
pairs.rationale: Same subject (error surface), mutually exclusive contracts.

a.summary: Tool errors return {error: str} dicts — never raise across the MCP boundary
a.detail: Raising loses the message in some clients.

b.summary: Tools raise typed exceptions; the dispatch layer converts to protocol errors
b.detail: Centralized conversion beats per-tool error dicts.

pairs.label: contradicts
pairs.rationale: Deployment-cadence stance conflict between peers.

a.summary: Ship every gated WP immediately — small releases, fast feedback

b.summary: Batch releases weekly — every-WP releases overloaded the pin/update pipeline
b.detail: Marketplace pin churn cost more than the feedback was worth.

pairs.label: contradicts
pairs.rationale: Data-retention stance conflict, both HEAD, cross-severity.

a.summary: Deleted node blobs must be purged from disk within 24h — privacy commitment

b.summary: Keep deleted-node blobs 90 days in a recovery area before purge
b.detail: Two accidental deletions were unrecoverable; retention window added.

pairs.label: contradicts
pairs.rationale: Modeled on the real graph's genuine conflict: same capability asserted honored vs not honored, both presented as current findings.

a.summary: Agent frontmatter model pins are honored when the plugin runs from --plugin-dir
a.detail: Verified in dev context: spawned analyzer ran on the pinned model.

b.summary: Installed-context run ignored the frontmatter model pin — analyzer inherited the session model
b.detail: Production evidence: cost spike traced to analyzer running on the session's large model despite the pin.
b.context: agent frontmatter, model pins, installed context

pairs.label: contradicts
pairs.rationale: Concurrency-model stance conflict on the same component.

a.summary: Dashboard endpoints stay sync handlers — Starlette's default threadpool is the concurrency model
a.detail: Uniform with siblings; no explicit executor management.

b.summary: Dashboard endpoints must be async with explicit run_in_threadpool for blocking work only
b.detail: Blanket sync handlers hide blocking hotspots; make blocking explicit.

pairs.label: supersedes
pairs.rationale: Same author, same subject, stance evolved with cited reason — lineage, not conflict.

a.summary: Cache embeddings in-process with an LRU of 512 entries

b.summary: Move embedding cache to the on-disk store — LRU thrashing measured at 40% miss rate; replaces the in-process LRU decision
b.detail: Measured on production workload; supersedes the 512-entry LRU choice.

pairs.label: supersedes
pairs.rationale: Explicit revision language ('replaces'), same subject, later node.

a.summary: Auth tokens live in a query parameter for the dashboard
a.detail: Simplest to wire for a loopback-only server.

b.summary: Dashboard auth moves to a header token; query-param tokens leaked into logs — replaces the query-param decision
b.detail: INFO logging captured full URLs.

pairs.label: supersedes
pairs.rationale: Version-bump of a procedure: newer workflow revises the same runbook.

a.summary: Release procedure v1: bump version, push, notify maintainer

b.summary: Release procedure v2: adds isolated-worktree gate + lockfile regen before push
b.detail: Same runbook, two steps added after a bad release.

pairs.label: supersedes
pairs.rationale: Team-level stance evolution with the old decision named; different author but explicit lineage.

a.summary: Support Python 3.10+ — widest plugin audience

b.summary: Floor moves to Python 3.12 — 3.10/3.11 CI images EOL'd; revises the 3.10-floor decision
b.detail: Names and updates the earlier floor decision after CI provider dropped images.

pairs.label: supersedes
pairs.rationale: Threshold retuned by the same author — evolution of the same knob.

a.summary: Duplicate-detection threshold set at cosine 0.90

b.summary: Duplicate threshold retuned to 0.84 after measuring misses on real duplicates
b.detail: 0.90 missed 6 of 9 labeled duplicates; retuned with data.

pairs.label: supersedes
pairs.rationale: fail retracting a discovery — the retraction shape (newer corrects older, same subject, cites it).

a.summary: ChromaDB persists fine across process kills — no explicit close needed

b.summary: Windows: chromadb file handles survive object deletion — the no-close-needed finding does not hold there; explicit close() required
b.detail: PermissionError on tempdir cleanup traced to open sqlite/HNSW handles; corrects the earlier finding for Windows.

pairs.label: none
pairs.rationale: Scope difference: same-looking rule, different modules — not a conflict.

a.summary: The journal file is append-only — no in-place edits ever

b.summary: Config file is edited in place with atomic rename
b.detail: Small file, atomic-rename is fine.

pairs.label: none
pairs.rationale: Refinement: B narrows A without negating it.

a.summary: Casefold emails before any comparison

b.summary: Casefold emails AND strip surrounding whitespace at the single normalization point
b.detail: Adds trimming to the established casefold rule.

pairs.label: none
pairs.rationale: Topic overlap without stance: two discoveries about the same subsystem, compatible.

a.summary: Embedding model takes ~250MB on first download
a.detail: Hugging Face fetch on first use.

b.summary: Embedding generation is 3x faster with input batching
b.detail: Batch of 32 vs singles.

pairs.label: none
pairs.rationale: Different subjects that share vocabulary (retries) — gateway policy vs curation retry, unrelated.

a.summary: Gateway retries idempotent upstream calls twice

b.summary: Curation batch that fails is retried once next run, not immediately
b.detail: Idempotent per batch; immediate retry wastes tokens.

pairs.label: none
pairs.rationale: Complementary constraints on the same file — both hold simultaneously.

a.summary: Nobody commits the journal on a WP branch

b.summary: Journal merges use the union merge driver
b.detail: Append-only file merges clean with union.

pairs.label: none
pairs.rationale: Old stance already superseded by a third node — B conflicts only with A's RETIRED stance; A is not HEAD (fixture marks A superseded).

a.summary: [SUPERSEDED by newer decision] Store per-project config in .mcp.json
a.detail: Original approach, later replaced by plugin-declared server config.

b.summary: The plugin declares its MCP server in plugin.json — no per-project .mcp.json
b.detail: Server config travels with the plugin.

pairs.label: none
pairs.rationale: Question-shaped vs stance: an assumption flagged for verification does not conflict with a decision pending that verification.

a.summary: Assuming the marketplace pin propagates to installs within an hour

b.summary: Release notes tell users to update manually rather than waiting for auto-pickup
b.detail: Removes dependence on propagation timing.

pairs.label: none
pairs.rationale: Same subsystem, sequential facts (one led to the other) — causal, not conflicting.

a.summary: Background heavy-import wedged in native DLL load, dispatch hung forever

b.summary: Heavy imports move to a probed subprocess with a timeout
b.detail: Response to the wedge.

pairs.label: none
pairs.rationale: Two prohibitions on different layers that read superficially opposite (allow vs deny) but govern different actors.

a.summary: Only the curate-orchestrator may write semantic edges

b.summary: Deterministic part_of edges are written automatically by the storage layer
b.detail: Shared-reference matching, not semantic.

pairs.label: none
pairs.rationale: Same metric, different environments — both true where they apply.

a.summary: Test suite runs in 140s on the dev machine

b.summary: Test suite runs in 320s on CI runners
b.detail: CI baseline, slower disks.

pairs.label: none
pairs.rationale: Hard negative for C02's shape: pool-size numbers differ but B explicitly operates within A's cap via a different mechanism (read replicas) — compatible.

a.summary: Primary DB connection pool capped at 10 by licence

b.summary: Reporting reads move to a read replica with its own pool of 40
b.detail: Primary stays within its cap of 10; replica is separately licensed.

pairs.label: none
pairs.rationale: Style disagreement without a binding stance: a pattern and a preference note that never mandates.

a.summary: Prefer dataclasses for config carriers

b.summary: Pydantic models give env-var parsing for free in Settings
b.detail: Observed convenience for settings specifically.

pairs.label: none
pairs.rationale: Temporal coincidence, shared release, no stance overlap.

a.summary: Shipped the identity node layer in v0.19.0

b.summary: Seniority vocabulary pinned to owner>senior>mid>junior
b.detail: Closed set for weighting.

pairs.label: none
pairs.rationale: Hard negative for retraction shape S06: B limits A's claim to a subset WITHOUT retracting it (both stand).

a.summary: Journal replay is safe on old client versions — type read as raw string

b.summary: Old clients' startup embedding SYNC (not replay) aborts on unknown node types
b.detail: Different code path than replay; replay stays safe.

pairs.label: none
pairs.rationale: Both nodes prohibit the same thing with different rationales — agreement, despite different wording.

a.summary: Never add a marketplace.json named coltondyck to this repo
a.detail: Name collision with the real marketplace.

b.summary: Marketplace metadata lives only in the marketplace repo; code repos ship code
b.detail: Separation of concerns for plugin distribution.

pairs.label: none
pairs.rationale: Trap pair: strongly opposed WORDING ('always' vs 'never') but subjects differ (logging levels vs log destinations).

a.summary: Always log at INFO for state-changing operations

b.summary: Never log to files inside .cognition — the dir is graph state, not a log sink
b.detail: Keeps the shared dir clean for replay.

pairs.label: none
pairs.rationale: Near-duplicate: two agents recorded the same stance independently — duplication, not conflict.

a.summary: Version bumps happen only on the WP branch, cut by the implementer after code sign-off
a.detail: Keeps main's history clean of orphan bumps.

b.summary: Implementer cuts the bump commit on the branch after the gate signs off code
b.detail: Same practice, recorded from the implementer's side.

pairs.label: none
pairs.rationale: Default-plus-override shape that mimics the value-pin conflict (C02 shape): B is an explicit escape hatch A anticipates, not a competing pin.

a.summary: Search timeout defaults to 30s
a.detail: Sensible default; callers may override per call.

b.summary: Long-running export jobs pass an explicit 300s timeout to search
b.detail: Uses the documented per-call override for the export path only.

pairs.label: none
pairs.rationale: Different environments: dev vs prod choices that read opposed but never govern the same deployment.

a.summary: Dev environments run SQLite — zero-setup local iteration

b.summary: Production runs Postgres with connection pooling
b.detail: Applies to the prod deployment.

pairs.label: none
pairs.rationale: An incident report and the standing rule it violated — the incident CONFIRMS the rule rather than opposing it.

a.summary: Gate runs happen in an isolated worktree, never the shared main tree
a.detail: Shared-tree checkouts have clobbered teammates' work.

b.summary: A checkout intended for the gate worktree ran in the shared main tree after a cwd reset
b.detail: Shell cwd reset between commands; the rule held, execution slipped.
